adopted_roadmap.fase_i_summary: 3 DGs shipped (DG-125 A + DG-125.0.1 + DG-126 A), 3 Baselines empíricos (9, 9b, 10), Baseline-10 PARTIAL PASS classification. R2 fix VERIFIED empíricamente, R1 infra shipped pero utility empírica INCONCLUSIVA por colony memory saturation + SCA-dominated scan composition. R3 misdiagnosed y honestly dropped. Duration real: 2 días vs estimate 2-3 semanas — velocity alta por incremental scope de trust foundations (no aplicar la mismo velocity a FASE II — paradigm change requires more baselines).
fase_ii_dgs_candidatos: DG-127 A R18 v2 symbol-level cross-file resolution (primer DG, EL SALTO DEL NORTH STAR)
DG-128 A cross-file taint propagation sobre R18 v2
DG-129 A R19 module trust boundary tagging (public-entry vs internal-handler vs test-fixture)

activeDG.title: R18 v2 symbol-level cross-file resolution — EL SALTO DEL NORTH STAR (primer DG de FASE II)
activeDG.north_star_use_case: SYNAPTIC_SAAS Baseline previa: sentinel-js-taint-sql-injection en agent.ts:62 con snippet `agentLoop.execute(orchestrationRequest)`. LLM produjo rationale 'without seeing the internals of agentLoop.execute(), cannot determine reachability' — v1 file-level graph no ayudó porque agentLoop.execute está definido en OTRO archivo. R18 v2 resolvería 'agentLoop from ../services/agent-loop.js' + attach 'execute() defined at agent-loop.ts:42 with signature: async execute(req: OrchestrationRequest): Promise<Result>'. LLM ahora puede razonar cross-file.
activeDG.predecessor_baseline_10_status: PARTIAL PASS — R2 verified, R1 shipped inconclusive; regression check clean. FASE I closed successfulCycles 114.
